title: Voicemail and Call Recording Retention Compliance
dataSources: Voicemail and recording metadata, retention policy events
app: UCM/Teams Call Quality, voicemail system logs
spl:
index=ucm sourcetype="voicemail:metadata"
| eval age_days=floor((now()-_time)/86400)
| where age_days > 365
| stats count by mailbox_id, retention_policy
| sort -count
description: Voicemail and call recordings may be subject to retention and deletion policies. Monitoring supports compliance and storage management.
value: Voicemail and call recordings may be subject to retention and deletion policies. Monitoring supports compliance and storage management.
implementation: Ingest voicemail and recording metadata. Compare against retention policy (e.g., 1 year). Alert on items past retention. Report on storage by policy and cleanup actions.
visualization: Table (items past retention), Single value (total over-retained), Bar chart (by mailbox).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: UCM/Teams Call Quality, voicemail system logs.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: Voicemail and recording metadata, retention policy events.
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Ingest voicemail and recording metadata. Compare against retention policy (e.g., 1 year). Alert on items past retention. Report on storage by policy and cleanup actions.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ucm sourcetype="voicemail:metadata"
| eval age_days=floor((now()-_time)/86400)
| where age_days > 365
| stats count by mailbox_id, retention_policy
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Voicemail and Call Recording Retention Compliance** ‚Äî Voicemail and call recordings may be subject to retention and deletion policies. Monitoring supports compliance and storage management.

Documented **Data sources**: Voicemail and recording metadata, retention policy events. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): UCM/Teams Call Quality, voicemail system logs. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ucm; **sourcetype**: voicemail:metadata. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=ucm, sourcetype="voicemail:metadata". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ `eval` defines or adjusts **age_days** ‚Äî often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where age_days > 365` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by mailbox_id, retention_policy** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (items past retention), Single value (total over-retained), Bar chart (by mailbox).
grandmaExplanation: Voicemail and call recordings may be subject to retention and deletion policies ‚Äî so phishing, data leaks, and account misuse do not slip through.
hardware: Cisco Unified Communications Manager (CUCM), Unity Connection, IP Phone 7800 series, IP Phone 8800 series
